# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/denoland/deploy_std@0.0.1/http/server.ts";

const indexHTML = `<html>
<body>
  <h1>Hello Deno Deploy!</h1>
</body>
</html>`;

const server = serve("0.0.0.0:8000");
console.log("http://0.0.0.0:8000/");

for await (const req of server) {
  const params = new URLSearchParams(req.url.substring(1));
  const moduleParam = params.get("module");
  const moduleEnv = Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH");
  const modulePath = moduleParam || moduleEnv;

  let result;
  if (modulePath) {
    const module = await import(modulePath);
    if (module.default && typeof module.default === 'function') {
      result = module.default();
    }
  }

  if (result) {
    req.respond({ body: result });
  } else {
    req.respond({ body: indexHTML });
  }
}
